full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: Part B adds task_tag, code_used_verbatim, and student_modification fields. For task_tag, can I use things like 'Task 3.1 Component 1'? And if I modified LLM code before using it, is code_used_verbatim true or false?
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Detailed task tags are fine. If I modified the code at all, code_used_verbatim should be false. Only set true if I copied code exactly as the LLM gave it.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Re-read the exam table which says 'true if any LLM-generated code was copied without modification.'

full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: For the top 5 prompts, should I pick the most technical ones or the ones that actually helped me the most? Some simple questions had more impact on my work than complex ones.
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Pick based on impact rather than complexity. A simple prompt that shaped the whole code architecture matters more than a tangential theoretical question.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Reviewed all my top_5_prompts selections across all files to ensure they reflect actual impact.
